description: HP/Agilent 8591E spectrum analyzer, 9 kHz to 1.8 GHz. Part of the 8590 E-series; uses the 8590-series command language (mnemonic-based, not SCPI). Most numeric commands accept a value with a unit suffix and an optional AUTO/UP/DN argument, and most are queryable by appending '?'.

identity.description: ID returns the spectrum analyzer model number (e.g. HP8591E) to the controller.

commands.name: preset
commands.description: Instrument preset. Returns the spectrum analyzer to a known state.

commands.name: base_band_preset
commands.description: Performs an instrument preset to the base band (band 0).

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Returns the spectrum analyzer model number to the controller (for example HP8591E).

commands.name: revision
commands.description: Returns the date code of the firmware revision number in YYMMDD format.

commands.name: serial_number
commands.description: Returns the serial number suffix of the spectrum analyzer.

commands.name: have_option
commands.description: Returns a 0 if a device or option is not installed, otherwise non-zero.

commands.name: done
commands.description: Allows you to determine when the spectrum analyzer has started to execute all commands prior to and including DONE.

commands.name: abort
commands.description: Stops the execution of all user-defined functions and readies the instrument for the next command received.

commands.name: wait
commands.description: Suspends all spectrum analyzer operation for the specified time duration.

commands.name: memory_available
commands.description: Returns the amount of spectrum analyzer memory available.

commands.name: power_up_time
commands.description: Returns the number of milliseconds that have elapsed since the spectrum analyzer was turned on.

commands.name: mode
commands.description: Returns 0 if the mode of operation is spectrum analysis; a non-zero value is returned if the operating mode (personality) is other than spectrum analysis.

commands.name: release_hpib
commands.description: Releases spectrum analyzer control of the HP-IB.

commands.name: center_frequency
commands.description: Specifies the center frequency. Default unit is Hz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: frequency range of the spectrum analyzer (to 1.8 GHz)

commands.name: frequency_span
commands.description: Changes the total displayed frequency range symmetrically about the center frequency. Default unit is Hz.

commands.name: start_frequency
commands.description: Specifies the start (left edge) frequency.

commands.name: stop_frequency
commands.description: Specifies the stop (right edge) frequency.

commands.name: full_span
commands.description: Sets the frequency span of the spectrum analyzer to full span.

commands.name: last_span
commands.description: Changes the spectrum analyzer span to the previous span setting.

commands.name: center_frequency_step
commands.description: Specifies the center-frequency step size used by the UP/DN step keys. Accepts AUTO to couple to span.

commands.name: frequency_offset
commands.description: Specifies the frequency offset for all absolute frequency readouts such as center frequency.

commands.name: harmonic_number
commands.description: Returns the harmonic number of the current harmonic band in which the spectrum analyzer is tuning.

commands.name: harmonic_number_lock
commands.description: Forces the spectrum analyzer to use only the selected harmonic band.

commands.name: harmonic_number_unlock
commands.description: Unlocks the harmonic band.

commands.name: marker_frequency_output
commands.description: Returns the frequency (or time) of the on-screen active marker.

commands.name: reference_level
commands.description: Specifies the amplitude value of the reference level. Default unit is the current amplitude unit. Preset 0 dBm.

parameters.name: level
parameters.range: amplitude range of the spectrum analyzer

commands.name: reset_reference_level
commands.description: Resets the reference level to its instrument preset level.

commands.name: reference_level_offset
commands.description: Offsets all amplitude readouts without affecting the trace.

commands.name: reference_level_position
commands.description: Selects the position of the reference level (used in normalization).

commands.name: normalized_reference_level
commands.description: Sets the normalized reference level.

commands.name: attenuation
commands.description: Specifies the RF input attenuation in 10 dB steps. Default unit dB. Accepts AUTO/UP/DN. Preset 10 dB.

parameters.name: attenuation
parameters.range: input attenuator range of the spectrum analyzer (0 to 70 dB)

commands.name: amplitude_units
commands.description: Specifies the amplitude units for input, output, and display.

parameters.name: units
parameters.range: DBM | DBMV | DBUV | DB | V | MV | UV | W | MW | UW

commands.name: log_scale
commands.description: Specifies the vertical graticule divisions as logarithmic units (dB/division), without changing the reference level.

commands.name: linear_scale
commands.description: Specifies the vertical graticule divisions as linear units, without changing the reference level.

commands.name: mixer_level
commands.description: Specifies the maximum signal level applied to the input mixer for a signal equal to or below the reference level.

commands.name: input_impedance
commands.description: Specifies the value of input impedance expected at the active input port (50 or 75 ohm).

commands.name: coupling
commands.description: Selects direct-current (DC) coupling or alternating-current (AC) coupling.

commands.name: external_preamp_gain
commands.description: Subtracts a positive or negative preamplifier gain value from the displayed signal.

commands.name: preselector_peak
commands.description: Performs a preselector peak.

commands.name: resolution_bandwidth
commands.description: Specifies the resolution bandwidth. Default unit Hz. Accepts AUTO to couple to span. Preset 3 MHz. Range 1 kHz to 3 MHz (30 Hz to 3 MHz with Option 130).

parameters.name: bandwidth
parameters.range: 1 kHz to 3 MHz (30 Hz to 3 MHz with Option 130)

commands.name: video_bandwidth
commands.description: Specifies the video bandwidth. Accepts AUTO to couple to resolution bandwidth.

commands.name: video_bandwidth_ratio
commands.description: Specifies the coupling ratio of video bandwidth to resolution bandwidth.

commands.name: video_average
commands.description: Turns on or off video averaging, which averages trace points to smooth the displayed trace.

parameters.name: count
parameters.range: ON, OFF, or number of averages

commands.name: clear_average
commands.description: Restarts (clears) video averaging.

commands.name: sweep_time
commands.description: Specifies the time in which the spectrum analyzer sweeps the displayed frequency range. Accepts AUTO to couple to span.

commands.name: take_sweep
commands.description: Starts and completes one full sweep before the next command is executed.

commands.name: single_sweep
commands.description: Selects single-sweep mode.

commands.name: continuous_sweep
commands.description: Sets the spectrum analyzer to the continuous-sweep mode.

commands.name: sweep_couple
commands.description: Selects a stimulus-response (SR) or spectrum-analyzer (SA) auto-coupled sweep time.

commands.name: auto_couple
commands.description: Couples the active functions automatically. Executing AUTO with no active function couples all functions.

commands.name: trigger_mode
commands.description: Selects a trigger mode: free run, line, video, TV, or external.

commands.name: on_end_of_sweep
commands.description: Executes the list of analyzer commands after the end of the sweep.

commands.name: on_sweep
commands.description: Executes the list of analyzer commands at the beginning of the sweep.

commands.name: on_marker
commands.description: Performs the list of analyzer commands when the sweep reaches the marker position.

commands.name: on_marker_update
commands.description: Executes the list of analyzer commands whenever the value or units of the active marker change.

commands.name: on_service_request
commands.description: Executes the list of analyzer commands whenever a service request occurs.

commands.name: on_time
commands.description: Executes the list of analyzer commands at the specified time.

commands.name: on_delay
commands.description: Executes the list of analyzer commands after the time value has elapsed.

commands.name: on_cycle
commands.description: Executes the list of analyzer commands periodically.

commands.name: detector
commands.description: Selects the spectrum analyzer detection mode: positive-peak, sample, or negative. Preset DET POS.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.range: POS | SMP | NEG (NEG requires Option 101 or 301)

commands.name: clear_write
commands.description: Clears the specified trace and enables trace data acquisition.

commands.name: view_trace
commands.description: Displays trace A, B, or C and stops taking new data into the viewed trace.

commands.name: blank_trace
commands.description: Blanks trace A, B, or C and stops taking new data into the specified trace.

commands.name: maximum_hold
commands.description: Updates trace elements with the maximum level detected (max hold).

commands.name: minimum_hold
commands.description: Updates trace C elements with the minimum level detected (min hold).

commands.name: trace_display
commands.description: Turns on or off the display of trace A, B, or C without clearing the trace (measurements can still be taken).

commands.name: trace_preset
commands.description: Sets the trace operations to their preset values.

commands.name: trace_status
commands.description: Returns the status of traces A, B, and C: clear write, blank, view, minimum hold, or maximum hold.

commands.name: trace_a_minus_b
commands.description: Subtracts trace B from trace A and sends the result to trace A during every sweep.

commands.name: trace_a_minus_b_plus_dl
commands.description: Subtracts trace B from trace A, adds the display line value, and sends the result to trace A during every sweep.

commands.name: trace_a_plus_b
commands.description: Adds trace A to trace B and sends the result to trace A.

commands.name: exchange_a_b
commands.description: Exchanges trace A and trace B.

commands.name: trace_b_minus_dl
commands.description: Subtracts the display line from trace B and places the result in trace B.

commands.name: transfer_b_to_c
commands.description: Transfers trace B into trace C.

commands.name: exchange_b_c
commands.description: Exchanges trace B and trace C.

commands.name: trace_data_format
commands.description: Formats trace information for return to the controller.

parameters.name: format
parameters.range: P (parameter/real), M (measurement units, ASCII), B (binary byte), A (binary word), I (image/instrument)

commands.name: transfer_trace_data
commands.description: Controls trace A data input or output (use TRA/TRB/TRC for traces A, B, C). Querying returns the trace amplitude values.

commands.name: transfer_trace_a
commands.description: Returns trace A amplitude values from the analyzer to the controller.

commands.name: transfer_trace_b
commands.description: Returns trace B amplitude values from the analyzer to the controller.

commands.name: trace_registers_available
commands.description: Returns a nonnegative integer indicating the total number of trace registers available for SAVET and RCLT.

commands.name: define_trace
commands.description: Creates (declares) a user-defined trace.

commands.name: graph_trace
commands.description: Displays a compressed trace on the analyzer display (graphs compressed trace).

commands.name: marker_peak_search
commands.description: Positions the active marker on a signal peak. MKPK HI finds the highest peak; NH next peak; NR next peak right; NL next peak left.

parameters.name: target
parameters.range: HI (highest), NH (next), NR (next right), NL (next left)

commands.name: marker_normal
commands.description: Activates and moves the marker to the specified frequency.

commands.name: marker_frequency
commands.description: Specifies the frequency value of the active marker.

commands.name: marker_amplitude
commands.description: Specifies or returns the amplitude of the active marker.

commands.name: marker_position
commands.description: Places the active marker at the given x-coordinate (trace position).

commands.name: marker_delta
commands.description: Activates the delta marker.

commands.name: marker_to_center_frequency
commands.description: Sets the center frequency equal to the marker frequency and moves the marker to the center of the screen.

commands.name: marker_to_reference_level
commands.description: Sets the reference level to the amplitude value of the active marker.

commands.name: marker_to_span
commands.description: Sets the start and stop frequencies to the values of the delta markers.

commands.name: marker_to_step_size
commands.description: Sets the center-frequency step size to the marker frequency.

commands.name: marker_minimum
commands.description: Moves the active marker to the minimum signal detected.

commands.name: marker_off
commands.description: Turns off either the active marker or all the markers (MKOFF ALL).

commands.name: marker_noise
commands.description: Displays the average noise level at the marker.

commands.name: marker_track
commands.description: Moves the signal with an active marker to the center of the display and keeps the signal peak at center screen.

commands.name: marker_trace
commands.description: Moves the active marker to a corresponding position in trace A, B, or C.

commands.name: marker_type
commands.description: Changes the type of the current active marker.

commands.name: marker_readout
commands.description: Selects the type of active trace information displayed by the marker readout (frequency, time, inverse-time, or period).

commands.name: marker_peak_excursion
commands.description: Specifies the minimum signal excursion for the internal peak-identification routine.

commands.name: marker_bandwidth
commands.description: Returns the bandwidth at the specified power level relative to an on-screen marker or the signal peak.

commands.name: marker_active
commands.description: Specifies the active marker (SELECT 1 2 3 4).

commands.name: marker_as_active_function
commands.description: Makes the current active marker the active function.

commands.name: marker_continue
commands.description: Resumes the sweep after execution of an MKSTOP command.

commands.name: marker_stop
commands.description: Stops the sweep at the active marker.

commands.name: marker_pause
commands.description: Pauses the sweep at the active marker for the duration of the delay period.

commands.name: marker_counter
commands.description: Turns on or off the marker frequency counter.

commands.name: marker_counter_resolution
commands.description: Sets the resolution of the marker frequency counter. Accepts AUTO.

commands.name: marker_table
commands.description: Turns on or off the marker table.

commands.name: marker_delta_dl_mode
commands.description: Selects whether marker amplitude values are shown relative to the reference level or relative to the display line.

commands.name: marker_zoom
commands.description: Activates a single marker on the trace and enables the knob to change the marker position; the active function is then set to span.

commands.name: display_line
commands.description: Defines the level of the display line in the active amplitude units and displays the display line on the screen. Accepts ON/OFF.

commands.name: threshold
commands.description: Clips signal responses below the threshold level. Accepts ON/OFF.

commands.name: graticule
commands.description: Turns on or off the graticule.

commands.name: annotation
commands.description: Turns on or off the screen annotation.

commands.name: title
commands.description: Activates the screen title mode (writes a title string on screen).

commands.name: menu
commands.description: Displays the specified softkey menu on the spectrum analyzer screen.

commands.name: clear_display
commands.description: Erases user-generated graphics and text.

commands.name: hold_data_entry
commands.description: Disables data entry via the keypad, knob, or step keys and blanks the active function readout.

commands.name: analog_plus
commands.description: Turns the Analog+ display mode on or off (Option 101 or 301).

commands.name: crt_horizontal_position
commands.description: Specifies the horizontal position of the text and graticule on the display.

commands.name: crt_vertical_position
commands.description: Specifies the vertical position of the text and graticule on the display.

commands.name: calibrate
commands.description: Initiates self-calibration routines.

commands.name: confidence_test
commands.description: Performs the confidence test.

commands.name: amplitude_correction
commands.description: Applies amplitude corrections at specified frequencies.

commands.name: amplitude_correction_length
commands.description: Returns the number of frequency-amplitude correction factors that have been entered.

commands.name: correction_factors_on
commands.description: Returns 1 if the correction factors are on, 0 if they are off.

commands.name: status_byte_query
commands.description: Returns to the controller the decimal equivalent of the status byte.

commands.name: clear_status_byte
commands.description: Clears all status bits.

commands.name: service_request_mask
commands.description: Sets a bit mask for service requests.

commands.name: force_service_request
commands.description: Used by an external controller to simulate interrupts from the spectrum analyzer.

commands.name: command_error_query
commands.description: Returns command error information.

commands.name: output_active_function
commands.description: Returns the value of the active function.

commands.name: output_parameter
commands.description: Returns the coordinates of the lower-left and upper-right corners of the display.

commands.name: output_learn_string
commands.description: Transmits information to the controller describing the state of the spectrum analyzer when OL is executed.

commands.name: user_state
commands.description: Transmits information that has been stored in the analyzer by the user.

commands.name: enable_entry
commands.description: Sends the controller the values entered on the numeric keypad by the operator; enables front-panel number entry.

commands.name: enable_knob
commands.description: Allows data entry with the front-panel knob when the spectrum analyzer is under remote control.

commands.name: enter_parameter
commands.description: Sends values entered on the numeric keyboard to the present active function value.

commands.name: enter_from_hpib
commands.description: Allows the spectrum analyzer to receive data from other devices on the HP-IB.

commands.name: output_to_port
commands.description: Allows the spectrum analyzer to send data to other devices on the HP-IB (or to the remote/parallel port).

commands.name: up
commands.description: Increases the active function by the applicable step size.

commands.name: down
commands.description: Reduces the active function by the applicable step size.

commands.name: save_state
commands.description: Saves the currently displayed instrument state in one of the state registers in spectrum analyzer memory.

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Recalls spectrum analyzer state data from one of the state registers in spectrum analyzer memory.

commands.name: save_trace
commands.description: Saves the selected trace data and state information, amplitude correction factors, or limit-line tables in spectrum analyzer memory.

commands.name: recall_trace
commands.description: Recalls previously saved trace data, amplitude factors, or limit-line data from the trace registers in spectrum analyzer memory.

commands.name: protect_state
commands.description: Protects all of the spectrum analyzer's user state and trace registers from being changed.

commands.name: mass_storage_is
commands.description: Specifies the current mass storage device as spectrum analyzer memory or a memory card.

commands.name: catalog
commands.description: Returns the catalog information of spectrum analyzer memory or the memory card.

commands.name: load
commands.description: Loads a file from the memory card.

commands.name: store
commands.description: Stores data on a RAM card.

commands.name: purge_file
commands.description: Deletes the specified file from the current mass storage device.

commands.name: prefix
commands.description: Specifies or changes the prefix used in save and recall operations.

commands.name: save_recall_number
commands.description: Specifies the number to append to the prefix for a save or recall operation and initiates the transfer of data.

commands.name: power_on_state
commands.description: Selects the spectrum analyzer power-on state: IP (instrument preset) or LAST.

commands.name: plot
commands.description: Initiates output of the spectrum analyzer display to a plotter.

commands.name: print
commands.description: Prints screen data to a printer.

commands.name: get_plot
commands.description: Initiates output of the spectrum analyzer display to a plotter (for use within a downloadable program).

commands.name: get_print
commands.description: Initiates output of the spectrum analyzer display to a printer (for use within a downloadable program).

commands.name: printer_address
commands.description: Sets the HP-IB address of the printer.

commands.name: adjacent_channel_power
commands.description: Performs the adjacent channel power measurement using channel spacing (ACPSP) and channel bandwidth (ACPBW).

commands.name: acp_extended
commands.description: Performs the adjacent channel power extended measurement.

commands.name: channel_power
commands.description: Performs the channel power measurement over the specified channel bandwidth (ACPBW).

commands.name: acp_channel_bandwidth
commands.description: Specifies the channel bandwidth used for the ACP, ACPE, and CHP measurements.

commands.name: acp_channel_spacing
commands.description: Specifies the frequency spacing between channels.

commands.name: acp_manual_auto
commands.description: Determines if the spectrum analyzer settings for ACP/ACPE/CHP/OBW measurements are set manually (0) or automatically (1).

commands.name: occupied_bandwidth
commands.description: Performs the occupied bandwidth measurement using the value for occupied bandwidth percent (OBWPCT).

commands.name: occupied_bandwidth_percent
commands.description: Specifies the percent of total power used in calculating the occupied bandwidth (OBW).

commands.name: percent_am
commands.description: Turns on or off the percent AM measurement.

commands.name: percent_am_response
commands.description: Returns the percent AM measured by the percent AM measurement (PCTAM).

commands.name: third_order_intermod
commands.description: Turns on (1) or off (0) the third-order intermodulation (TOI) measurement.

commands.name: third_order_intermod_response
commands.description: Returns the highest third-order intermodulation product measured by the TOI measurement.

commands.name: n_db_points
commands.description: Turns on or off the N dB points measurement.

commands.name: n_db
commands.description: Specifies the distance (in dB) from the signal peak for the N dB points measurement.

commands.name: n_db_points_bandwidth
commands.description: Returns the bandwidth measured by the N dB points measurement.

commands.name: power_bandwidth
commands.description: Computes the bandwidth around the trace center that includes signals whose total power is a specified percentage of the total trace signal power.

commands.name: measure_off
commands.description: Turns off the current measurement, erases the display, and then displays the MEAS/USER menu.

commands.name: fft
commands.description: Performs a discrete fast Fourier transform on the source trace array and stores the result in the destination array.

commands.name: peaks
commands.description: Sorts signal peaks by frequency or amplitude, stores the results in the destination trace, and returns the number of peaks found.

commands.name: demodulation
commands.description: Turns the demodulator on or off and selects between AM, FM, or quasi-peak demodulation.

commands.name: fm_gain
commands.description: Sets the total FM frequency deviation for full screen demodulation.

commands.name: speaker
commands.description: Turns on or off the internal speaker.

commands.name: squelch
commands.description: Sets the squelch level.

commands.name: control_line_a
commands.description: Sets the control line A of the auxiliary interface high or low.

commands.name: control_line_b
commands.description: Sets the control line B of the auxiliary interface high or low.

commands.name: control_line_c
commands.description: Sets the interface control line C of the auxiliary interface high or low.

commands.name: control_line_d
commands.description: Sets the interface control line D of the auxiliary interface high or low.

commands.name: control_line_input
commands.description: Returns a 1 when the interface control line I of the auxiliary interface is high, and 0 if the line is low.

commands.name: source_power
commands.description: Selects the source power level (tracking generator).

commands.name: source_tracking
commands.description: Adjusts tracking of source output with spectrum-analyzer sweep.

commands.name: source_tracking_peak
commands.description: Adjusts tracking of source output with spectrum-analyzer sweep (auto peak).

commands.name: source_power_offset
commands.description: Offsets the source power level readout.

commands.name: source_power_step
commands.description: Selects the source-power step size.

commands.name: source_power_sweep
commands.description: Selects sweep range of the source output (power sweep).

commands.name: source_attenuator
commands.description: Attenuates the source output level.

commands.name: source_leveling_control
commands.description: Selects internal or external leveling for the tracking generator.

commands.name: source_normalization
commands.description: Subtracts trace B from trace A, adds the display line, and sends the result to trace A (stimulus-response normalization).

commands.name: limit_line_test
commands.description: Compares trace A with the current limit-line data and enables limit-line testing.

commands.name: limits_failed
commands.description: Returns 0 if the last measurement sweep of trace A is equal to or within the limit-line bounds.

commands.name: limit_line_display
commands.description: Controls when the limit line (or limit lines) are displayed.

commands.name: delete_limit_line
commands.description: Deletes all segments in the current limit-line table.

commands.name: limit_line_segment_freq
commands.description: Adds new segments to the current frequency limit line in either the upper or lower limit line.

commands.name: limit_line_segment_time
commands.description: Adds new segments to the current sweep-time limit line in either the upper or lower limit line.

commands.name: limit_line_entry_mode
commands.description: Determines whether the limit-line entries are treated as upper, lower, upper-and-lower, or mid/delta amplitude values.

commands.name: limit_line_relative
commands.description: Specifies the current limit lines as fixed or relative.

commands.name: limit_line_freq_time
commands.description: Selects how the limit-line segments are placed on the display: according to frequency, or according to the sweep time setting.
